why_this_role.label: backend_distributed
why_this_role.template: This role lines up with the work I do best: building backend systems that have to stay reliable under load. A lot of my recent work has been event-driven and latency-sensitive, so I’m especially interested in roles where backend design, service boundaries, and operational resilience matter.

why_this_role.label: data_streaming
why_this_role.template: I’m interested in roles where data movement and system reliability are core to the product. I’ve built ingestion services, streaming workflows, and the monitoring needed to keep them healthy in production, so the fit here feels direct.

why_this_role.label: fintech
why_this_role.template: I’ve spent much of my career in financial and data-sensitive environments, where correctness, auditability, and reliability matter as much as raw throughput. That makes this role a natural fit for my background.

why_this_company.label: product_company
why_this_company.template: I’m drawn to companies where engineering work has a clear product impact. I like environments where backend systems are close to customer value, and where reliability and iteration speed both matter.

why_this_company.label: infrastructure_company
why_this_company.template: I’m especially interested in companies that treat infrastructure and backend systems as strategic advantages. That kind of environment tends to reward the kind of work I’ve focused on: scalability, fault tolerance, and performance under production load.

ambiguity_example.template: One example was an access-control platform I worked on at Citi. The requirements evolved as we worked through how approval workflows, entitlements, and service interactions needed to behave in production. I handled that by tightening service contracts, breaking the workflow into clear backend responsibilities, and using observability to validate how the system behaved as the design matured.

ownership_example.template: A strong ownership example was the access-control platform I built at Citi. I owned core backend design choices, API contracts, and the operational behavior of the system. That included making tradeoffs around event handling, reliability patterns, and production diagnostics rather than just implementing a narrow piece of the stack.
